recipes.name: Jason's Breakfast Smoothie
method: Add all ingredients to blender
Blend until smooth
Pour and drink
recipes.notes: Must finish by 8:15am (shower constraint). 15-20 min prep max. High-protein focus to hit daily 150g+ target.

recipes.name: Penny's Overnight Oats
method: Mix oats, milk, and Greek yogurt in a container/jar
Stir well to combine
Cover and refrigerate overnight
In the morning: top with baked strawberries (grab from fridge)
Eat straight from the fridge — no heating needed!
recipes.notes: Weekly prep: bake strawberries 400g at 180°C for 20-25 mins (makes ~5 servings). Perfect for Penny's 7:00am grab-and-go breakfast. Can leave home by 7:15am.

recipes.name: Ham, Cheese & Tomato Toastie
method: Butter outside of bread slices
Layer inside: ham, cheese, tomato slices
Heat sandwich press or pan over medium heat
Toast until bread is golden and cheese is melted (3-4 mins in press, 2-3 mins per side in pan)
Cut diagonally and serve
recipes.notes: Quick lunch option. Protein a bit lower than usual lunch targets — pairs well with a latte to boost protein. Can add side salad for extra vegetables.

recipes.name: Penny's Lunch Protein Shake
method: Add all ingredients to blender
Blend until smooth
Pour into bottle or glass
Drink immediately or store in fridge (consume within 24 hours)
recipes.notes: Variations: Classic Banana PB (~450 kcal, 45g P), Green Smoothie with spinach (~360 kcal, 33g P), Berry Blast with mixed berries (~370 kcal, 35g P). Can prep smoothie packs in freezer bags.

recipes.name: Beef Mince Bolognese
method: Heat oil in large pot, brown beef mince in batches
Set browned mince aside
In same pot, sauté diced onions, carrots until soft (8-10 mins)
Add garlic, cook 1 minute
Return mince to pot
Add canned tomatoes, tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce, herbs
Optional: add beef stock if you want more sauce
Simmer 30-45 minutes until thick and rich, stirring occasionally
Season with salt and pepper to taste
Cool completely before portioning
recipes.notes: Makes 6-7 Jason serves OR 8-10 Penny serves. Freezes well — keeps 2 days in fridge, 3 months in freezer. Serve with brown rice. Classic comfort food.

recipes.name: Beef Strips Stir Fry
method: Slice all vegetables, mince garlic
Heat wok/large pan on high heat with oil
Cook beef in batches (don't overcrowd!) for 2-3 mins until seared — set aside
Add onion first, cook 2 mins
Add capsicum and mushrooms, cook 4-5 mins
Add garlic, cook 1 min
Add spinach last, just until wilted
Return beef to pan
Add soy sauce, oyster sauce (if using), sesame oil
Toss everything together for 1-2 mins
Serve over brown rice or on its own
recipes.notes: Quick weeknight dinner: 20-25 minutes total. High heat is key — don't overcrowd the pan. Divide stir-fry 60/40 for Jason/Penny portions. Total cooked stir-fry: ~790g (before rice).

recipes.name: Chicken Savoury Mince
method: Brown 3kg chicken mince in batches in a large pot/wok
Set browned mince aside
In the same pot, sauté diced onions, carrots, celery, mushrooms, garlic until soft (about 10 mins)
Return mince to pot
Add soy sauce (season generously to taste)
Add sesame oil
Cook until vegetables are soft and mince is fully cooked through (15-20 mins)
Add spinach at the end, stir until just wilted
Cook brown rice in chicken stock instead of water (ratio: ~3 cups stock to 1 cup rice)
Cool completely before portioning
recipes.notes: This is a DRY mince — not gravy-based. Asian-style flavor profile with soy and sesame. No tomatoes, no gravy powder. Makes 8-12 serves. Freeze 7-10 serves. Keeps 2 days in fridge, 3 months in freezer.

recipes.name: Chicken Thighs with Rice & Broccoli
method: Season thighs with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and herbs
Heat oven to 200°C
Roast chicken thighs on a tray for 35-40 minutes until skin is crispy and internal temp reaches 75°C
Let rest 5 minutes before serving
Cook brown rice in chicken stock (bring to boil, reduce to simmer, cover 40-45 mins)
Steam or blanch broccoli for 3-4 minutes until tender-crisp
Season broccoli with salt, pepper, and a small drizzle of olive oil
Plate rice, top with chicken thigh(s), add broccoli on the side
recipes.notes: Can batch cook chicken thighs on weekend. Keeps 2 days in fridge. For meal prep: cook 9 thighs (6 for Jason, 3 for Penny across 3 meals). Optional: add purchased dahl for extra flavor.

recipes.name: Lemon Herb Salmon with Asparagus
method: Season salmon fillets with lemon juice, lemon zest, minced garlic, herbs, salt, and pepper
Let marinate for 10-15 minutes (optional)
Heat pan with olive oil over medium-high heat
Pan-fry salmon 4 minutes each side (skin side down first for crispy skin)
While salmon cooks, blanch or steam asparagus until tender-crisp (3-4 mins)
Cut cherry tomatoes in half
Plate: salmon, asparagus, cherry tomatoes, cottage cheese on the side
Grate fresh Parmesan over the top
Season with salt and pepper to taste
recipes.notes: Monday dinner staple (after Pilates). Quick prep: 30 minutes total. Salmon should be cooked medium (slightly pink in center). Alternative: bake everything on one tray at 200°C for 15-20 mins.

recipes.name: Prawns with Salad
method: If prawns are frozen, thaw in refrigerator overnight
Prepare salad: wash and chop vegetables
Make dressing: mix olive oil, lemon juice, salt, pepper
Assemble: place salad on plate, top with prawns
Drizzle with dressing
Serve immediately
recipes.notes: Very low calorie, high protein option. No cooking required if using pre-cooked prawns. 1kg prawns = ~4 Jason servings OR 7-8 Penny servings. Light option — may need additional snacks to hit calorie targets. Can add avocado for healthy fats.
